metadata.archetype_justification: The application coordinates multi-step workflow execution by scheduling tasks to downstream workers, managing state transitions via a DeciderService, and reconciling workflow state across multiple steps. It is a workflow orchestration engine by design.
metadata.context: Workflow orchestration engine originally from Netflix.

top_gaps.title: API Authentication
top_gaps.gap: No authentication or authorization on any API endpoint
top_gaps.impact: Critical security vulnerability — server is completely open to any caller

top_gaps.title: Infrastructure as Code Coverage
top_gaps.gap: No IaC files exist (no Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, Helm)
top_gaps.impact: Cannot reproduce infrastructure; manual/ClickOps deployment; no DR capability

top_gaps.title: Secrets Management
top_gaps.gap: All secrets via environment variables with no managed secrets service
top_gaps.impact: No rotation, no audit trail, credential sprawl risk

top_gaps.title: Managed Compute
top_gaps.gap: No managed compute defined; only Docker Compose for local dev
top_gaps.impact: Cannot scale, no auto-healing, no production-grade deployment

top_gaps.title: Distributed Tracing
top_gaps.gap: No tracing SDK or trace propagation across service boundaries
top_gaps.impact: Cannot diagnose latency or failures in distributed workflow execution

quick_agent_wins.name: DevOps Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: CI/CD pipeline exists (INF-Q11 = 3). GitHub Actions CI workflows are well-structured.
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An agent that triggers deployments, checks build status, manages releases, and monitors pipeline health.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Expose GitHub Actions API or integrate with a webhook-based trigger mechanism.

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists in the repository (README files, JSON schemas, proto definitions, configuration docs).
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: A knowledge agent that indexes workflow definition schemas, API documentation, and configuration reference.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Generate a static OpenAPI spec from the running server to use as the primary corpus.

quick_agent_wins.name: Workflow Automation Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Workflow orchestration in place (INF-Q3 = 2). The system IS a workflow orchestrator.
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: An agent that monitors workflow execution states, identifies stuck workflows, triggers retries.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Expose the existing REST API (/api/workflow) as an agent tool surface.

decomposition_strategy.rationale: The monolith has well-defined module boundaries (48 Gradle subprojects). Pluggable architecture with clean interfaces makes incremental extraction feasible.
extraction_priority: AI Module - clearest bounded context with independent dependencies
Event Queue Modules (Kafka, SQS, AMQP) - independent messaging adapters

findings.title: No managed compute infrastructure defined
findings.description: No managed compute infrastructure is defined. The application is deployed via Docker Compose for local development and Docker images are published to Docker Hub (multi-arch). No ECS, EKS, Lambda, or Fargate configuration exists.
findings.gap: All compute is self-managed via Docker containers with no managed orchestration.
findings.recommendation: Deploy to Amazon EKS with managed node groups. Create Kubernetes Deployment manifests for the conductor-server container image.

findings.title: All databases self-managed
findings.description: All databases are self-managed via Docker Compose: PostgreSQL 16, MySQL, Redis 6.2.3, Elasticsearch 7.17.11, OpenSearch. No managed database resources defined.
findings.gap: No automated failover, no managed backups, no patching automation for any database.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to Aurora PostgreSQL, ElastiCache for Redis, and Amazon OpenSearch Service.

findings.title: Internal workflow coordination uses custom code
findings.description: The application IS a workflow orchestrator but its internal coordination uses custom code (WorkflowReconciler, DeciderService) rather than a dedicated external orchestration service.
findings.gap: Internal coordination patterns are implemented as custom code rather than leveraging a dedicated orchestration service.
findings.recommendation: For operational workflows around the system itself (deployment orchestration, data migration), consider AWS Step Functions. The application's core orchestration function is appropriately custom.

findings.title: Self-managed messaging for orchestration fan-out
findings.description: The system supports Kafka, SQS, AMQP, NATS as pluggable event queues. However, default deployment uses self-managed Redis queues. Only the SQS module uses a managed service.
findings.gap: For an orchestrator archetype, managed messaging should dominate. Default deployment relies on self-managed Redis queues.
findings.recommendation: Adopt Amazon SQS as the primary event queue for production. Use Amazon MSK Serverless instead of self-managed Kafka.

findings.title: No network security configuration
findings.description: No VPC, security groups, subnets, or NACLs defined. Docker Compose exposes ports directly with no network isolation.
findings.gap: No production network security. Services would be exposed without segmentation.
findings.recommendation: Define VPC with private subnets for application and database tiers. Use security groups with least-privilege rules.

findings.title: Minimal API entry point (nginx in container)
findings.description: Nginx serves as reverse proxy for UI in Docker but there is no managed API Gateway, ALB, or CloudFront with throttling, auth, or validation.
findings.gap: Load balancer present but minimal configuration — no auth, no throttling, no WAF.
findings.recommendation: Deploy API Gateway in front of the EKS service. Configure throttling, request validation, and authentication.

findings.title: No auto-scaling configuration
findings.description: No auto-scaling exists. Docker Compose defines single instances. No ASG, HPA, or application auto-scaling policies.
findings.gap: All capacity is statically provisioned. Cannot respond to traffic spikes.
findings.recommendation: Configure HPA on EKS based on custom metrics (workflow queue depth, task backlog). Configure Aurora auto-scaling.

findings.title: No backup or recovery configuration
findings.description: No backup configuration. Docker Compose volumes use local driver with no backup strategy.
findings.gap: No automated backups for any data store. Failure would result in complete data loss.
findings.recommendation: Enable Aurora automated backups with PITR. Configure ElastiCache backup. Enable OpenSearch automated snapshots.

findings.title: No high availability or multi-AZ deployment
findings.description: No multi-AZ configuration. Single instances deployed via Docker Compose.
findings.gap: Single instance deployment — AZ failure takes down the entire system.
findings.recommendation: Deploy EKS across 2+ AZs with pod anti-affinity. Use Aurora Multi-AZ. Configure ElastiCache Multi-AZ.

findings.title: Zero Infrastructure as Code coverage
findings.description: No IaC files exist. No Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, Helm charts, or Kustomize. All infrastructure is ClickOps.
findings.gap: Cannot reproduce infrastructure. Cannot perform DR. Cannot track infrastructure changes.
findings.recommendation: Create Terraform modules for EKS cluster, Aurora, ElastiCache, VPC networking, API Gateway, IAM, CloudWatch.

findings.title: CI/CD covers build and publish but not deployment
findings.description: GitHub Actions provides automated build, test, multi-arch Docker publish, and Maven Central publishing. No deployment automation to production environments.
findings.gap: No deployment to target environment. No IaC pipeline. No automated rollback.
findings.recommendation: Extend pipelines with deployment stages to EKS. Add Terraform plan/apply stages.

findings.title: Modular monolith with shared database schemas
findings.description: 48 Gradle subprojects with clear module boundaries, but all compile into a single Spring Boot JAR. Shared database schemas between modules.
findings.gap: Single deployable unit. Cannot independently scale or deploy modules.
findings.recommendation: Begin Strangler Fig extraction starting with the AI module.

findings.title: Primarily synchronous fan-out for task distribution
findings.description: For an orchestrator archetype, async should dominate. Default deployment uses synchronous Redis polling for task distribution. Workers poll via HTTP.
findings.gap: Primary fan-out mechanism relies on workers synchronously polling the server via HTTP.
findings.recommendation: Make Amazon SQS the default event queue. Implement EventBridge for workflow lifecycle events.

findings.title: Reconciler uses fixed-interval sweep rather than event-driven
findings.description: Workflows are handled asynchronously with persistent state and queue-based reconciliation. Minor gap: reconciler uses fixed-interval polling (500ms) rather than event-driven triggers.
findings.gap: Reconciler relies on fixed-interval sweep rather than event-driven triggers, introducing latency.
findings.recommendation: Replace fixed-interval reconciler with event-driven triggers (SQS message → immediate evaluation).

findings.title: No API versioning strategy
findings.description: All endpoints use flat /api/<resource> paths. No URL versioning, headers, or query parameters.
findings.gap: No versioning — breaking changes would affect all consumers simultaneously.
findings.recommendation: Introduce URL-based API versioning (e.g., /api/v1/workflow). Use API Gateway for version routing.

findings.title: Environment variables for endpoints, no dynamic discovery
findings.description: Environment variables and Docker DNS for inter-service communication. No dynamic service discovery (Cloud Map, Consul).
findings.gap: No dynamic discovery mechanism. If deployed to EKS, Kubernetes DNS would provide this.
findings.recommendation: Leverage Kubernetes DNS on EKS. Use AWS Cloud Map for cross-cluster communication.

findings.title: Document access is filesystem-based by default
findings.description: S3 integration exists for external storage but document access in the AI module is filesystem-based by default.
findings.gap: Data in managed storage (S3 option exists) but default is local filesystem with no parsing pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Make S3 the default external storage backend. Leverage Tika integration for parsing pipeline.

findings.title: Mostly centralized data access with AI module exception
findings.description: Pluggable persistence with DAO interfaces. AI module vector DB access is a separate pattern.
findings.gap: Mostly centralized; AI module's vector database access does not follow main persistence pattern.
findings.recommendation: Maintain current DAO pattern. AI module isolation becomes natural when extracted as a service.

findings.title: No audit logging
findings.description: No CloudTrail or audit logging configured. Only Log4j2 console appender for application logs.
findings.gap: Cannot trace who performed what actions. No immutable log storage.
findings.recommendation: Enable CloudTrail. Configure application-level audit logging for workflow CRUD operations. Use S3 with Object Lock.

findings.title: No encryption at rest
findings.description: No encryption configured on any data store. Elasticsearch security explicitly disabled.
findings.gap: No encryption at rest for workflow data, task inputs/outputs, or metadata.
findings.recommendation: Enable Aurora encryption with KMS, ElastiCache encryption, OpenSearch encryption. Use SSE-KMS for S3.

findings.title: No API authentication
findings.description: No Spring Security, no OAuth2/JWT, no API keys. Server is completely open.
findings.gap: All API endpoints open without authentication. Critical vulnerability.
findings.recommendation: Deploy API Gateway with Cognito authorizer. Add Spring Security with OAuth2 resource server.

findings.title: No identity provider integration
findings.description: No Cognito, Okta, OIDC, or SAML. Application has no authentication at all.
findings.gap: Cannot authenticate users or integrate with organizational SSO.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with Amazon Cognito. Configure OIDC federation. Define RBAC roles.

findings.title: Secrets in environment variables without rotation
findings.description: All secrets via environment variable interpolation. No plaintext in source. No Secrets Manager or Vault.
findings.gap: No rotation, no audit trail, no centralized management.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with AWS Secrets Manager. Configure automatic rotation. Use EKS Secrets Store CSI Driver.

findings.title: No compute hardening or vulnerability scanning
findings.description: Dockerfile uses debian:stable-slim with no hardening. No vulnerability scanning. Proactive CVE patches in build.gradle.
findings.gap: No container image scanning. No hardened base images. No runtime patching strategy.
findings.recommendation: Switch to hardened base image (distroless/Temurin Alpine). Add Trivy scanning in CI. Use Bottlerocket for EKS nodes.

findings.title: Partial security scanning (SonarCloud only)
findings.description: SonarCloud configured in CI. Proactive CVE patching in build.gradle. No SAST, no container scanning, no Dependabot.
findings.gap: No dedicated SAST, no container scanning, no automated dependency scanning.
findings.recommendation: Add Dependabot or Snyk. Add Trivy for container scanning. Add yarn audit for UI.

findings.title: No distributed tracing
findings.description: No OpenTelemetry SDK, X-Ray, Jaeger, or Zipkin. Only OTLP metrics export via Micrometer (not traces).
findings.gap: Cannot trace request flows across workflow execution lifecycle.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenTelemetry Java Agent for auto-instrumentation. Configure OTLP exporter to X-Ray via ADOT Collector.

findings.title: No SLO definitions
findings.description: Prometheus metrics with percentile tracking but no formal SLO definitions or error budgets.
findings.gap: No SLOs for workflow execution latency, task scheduling, or API response time.
findings.recommendation: Define SLOs for critical journeys: workflow latency, task scheduling latency, API p99.

findings.title: No business metrics
findings.description: Infrastructure metrics only via Micrometer. No workflow completion rates, task failure rates, or worker utilization.
findings.gap: No business outcome metrics that drive informed operational decisions.
findings.recommendation: Publish custom metrics: workflows started/completed/failed, task scheduling latency, worker utilization.

findings.title: No alerting or anomaly detection
findings.description: Prometheus metrics exposed but no alerting rules, no CloudWatch alarms, no anomaly detection.
findings.gap: No alerting — operational issues discovered only through manual observation.
findings.recommendation: Configure CloudWatch alarms on error rate, workflow failure rate, queue depth, connection pool exhaustion.

findings.title: No canary or blue/green deployment strategy
findings.description: Docker images published with version tags and HEALTHCHECK but no traffic shifting, no canary, no automated rollback.
findings.gap: Direct-to-production deployment pattern with basic health checks only.
findings.recommendation: Use ArgoCD with Argo Rollouts for canary deployments on EKS. Configure automatic rollback on error rate increase.

findings.title: Integration tests exist with minor gaps
findings.description: Integration tests in test-harness with Testcontainers. E2E tests. LocalStack for SQS/S3. Runs in CI as separate job.
findings.gap: Integration tests for primary workflows; no gRPC contract tests, no performance testing in CI.
findings.recommendation: Add gRPC contract tests. Add performance regression tests. Consider consolidating test-harness into main CI.

findings.title: No incident response automation or runbooks
findings.description: No runbooks, SSM documents, Lambda remediation, or self-healing patterns.
findings.gap: Incident response is entirely ad hoc. No automated remediation.
findings.recommendation: Create runbooks for stuck workflow recovery, queue backlog remediation, database failover. Automate via SSM.

findings.title: Ad hoc observability without ownership
findings.description: Metrics exposed via Prometheus endpoint but no dashboards, no named alarm owners, no team attribution.
findings.gap: Ad hoc observability — no clear ownership, no dashboards, no escalation paths.
findings.recommendation: Create CloudWatch/Grafana dashboards. Assign alarm owners. Add CODEOWNERS for observability config.

findings.title: No resource tagging governance
findings.description: No IaC exists so no resources to tag. No tagging standard documented.
findings.gap: Cannot track costs, identify ownership, or enforce budget controls.
findings.recommendation: Establish mandatory tagging policy when creating IaC: Environment, Service, Team, CostCenter, ManagedBy.

immediate_actions: Extract AI module as independent service

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1=1 meets threshold but Dockerfiles and container definitions already exist. Contextual guard blocks: application is already containerized.

triggering_questions.note: Score < 3 but contextual guard blocks — containers already exist (Dockerfile, docker-compose)

pathways.not_triggered_reason: Workload is already containerized (multi-stage Dockerfile, multi-arch Docker images published). The gap is managed orchestration (EKS/ECS), not containerization itself.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4=4 (no stored procedures). No commercial DB engines detected — all backends are open source (PostgreSQL, MySQL, Redis, Elasticsearch, OpenSearch).

triggering_questions.note: Score >= 3; no proprietary SQL or stored procedures

pathways.not_triggered_reason: All database engines already open source. No commercial engines (Oracle, SQL Server) detected.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q2=1 (all databases self-managed in Docker Compose)

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q4=2 but contextual guard blocks — no data processing/analytics workloads detected. The system is a workflow orchestrator, not a data processing pipeline.

triggering_questions.note: Score < 3 but no analytics/ETL workloads exist to migrate

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No data processing workloads exist. The system orchestrates workflows but does not perform ETL, analytics, or data lake operations.

immediate_actions: Create Terraform modules for full infrastructure

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: AI frameworks already extensively present: Spring AI 1.1.2, 12 LLM providers (Bedrock, OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.), MCP support, vector databases (pgvector, Pinecone, MongoDB).
pathways.not_triggered_reason: AI/agent frameworks are already extensively integrated. The AI module has 116 Java files covering 12 LLM providers, MCP (Model Context Protocol), vector databases, and RAG patterns. The primary trigger (no AI frameworks) is not met.
